  name = route |> Route.icon_atom() |> Atom.to_string() |> String.replace("_", "-")
  svg("icon-#{name}-#{size}.svg")
end
def line_icon(%Route{external_agency_name: "Massport", name: name}, _) when is_binary(name) do
  route_number = String.slice(name, 0..1)

  if route_number in Dotcom.TripPlan.Helpers.massport_icon_names() do
    svg("icon-massport-#{route_number}.svg")
  else
    report_missing_icon("route Massport #{route_number}")
    svg("icon-mode-bus-default.svg")
  end
end
def line_icon(%Route{external_agency_name: "Logan Express", name: name}, _)
    when is_binary(name) do
  if name in Dotcom.TripPlan.Helpers.logan_express_icon_names() do
    svg("icon-logan-express-#{name}.svg")
  else
    report_missing_icon("route Logan Express #{name}")
    svg("icon-mode-bus-default.svg")
  end
end
def line_icon(%Route{} = route, size) do
  route |> Route.icon_atom() |> mode_icon(size)
end

def static_attributes(path) do
  {static_path, static_integrity} = DotcomWeb.Endpoint.static_lookup(path)
  static_url = DotcomWeb.Endpoint.static_url() <> static_path

  href_or_src =
    if String.starts_with?(path, "/css") do
      :href
    else
      :src
    end

  %{integrity: static_integrity, crossorigin: "anonymous"} |> Map.put(href_or_src, static_url)
end
elixir_type: (binary() -> open_map())

def get_mbta_url_path(nil) do
  nil
end
def get_mbta_url_path(path) do
  uri = URI.parse(path)

  if uri.host != nil and String.contains?(uri.host, "mbta.com") do
    uri.path
  else
    nil
  end
end

definition:
def get_project_url_paths(s) do
  if String.contains?(s, "/projects") and String.contains?(s, "/update") do
    [s, Regex.replace(~r/\/update.*/, s, "")]
  else
    [s]
  end
end
elixir_type: (binary() -> empty_list() or non_empty_list(binary(), empty_list()))

definition:
def inject_alerts_section({:safe, rewritten}, {:safe, alerts_section}) do
  {:ok, parsed_rewritten} = Floki.parse_fragment(rewritten)

  case parsed_rewritten do
    [{"figure", _, _} = figure | rest] ->
      {:safe, [Floki.raw_html([figure]) | alerts_section] ++ [Floki.raw_html(rest)]}

    _ ->
      {:safe, alerts_section ++ [rewritten]}
  end
end
elixir_type: (dynamic(), dynamic() -> dynamic())

def setup_list_cta(list, conn_path) do
  current_path = ["" | conn_path] |> Enum.join("/")

  case list.cta do
    %{text: nil, url: nil} -> default_list_cta(list.ingredients.type, current_path)
    link_parts -> custom_list_cta(list.ingredients.type, link_parts, current_path)
  end
end

def do_svg_icon_with_circle({:safe, _} = icon, %__MODULE__{
      aria_hidden?: false,
      show_tooltip?: false
    }) do
  icon
end
def do_svg_icon_with_circle({:safe, _} = icon, %__MODULE__{} = args) do
  attrs = [aria: aria_attrs(args), data: data_attrs(args), title: title_attr(args)]
  Tag.content_tag(:span, [icon], attrs)
end
elixir_type: (dynamic(), %{:icon => dynamic(), :size => :default or :small, :show_tooltip? => :false or :true, :aria_hidden? => :false or :true, :__struct__ => :"DotcomWeb.PartialView.SvgIconWithCircle"} -> dynamic())

definition:
def svg_icon_with_circle(%__MODULE__{icon: %Route{}} = args) do
  args.icon |> ViewHelpers.line_icon(args.size) |> do_svg_icon_with_circle(args)
end
def svg_icon_with_circle(%__MODULE__{icon: :mattapan_trolley} = args) do
  svg_icon_with_circle(%{args | icon: %Route{id: "Mattapan", type: 0}})
end
def svg_icon_with_circle(%__MODULE__{icon: line} = args)
    when line in [:red_line, :orange_line, :blue_line, :green_line, :mattapan_line] do
  route_id = line |> Atom.to_string() |> String.replace("_line", "") |> String.capitalize()
  [type] = Route.types_for_mode(line)
  svg_icon_with_circle(%{args | icon: %Route{id: route_id, type: type}})
end
def svg_icon_with_circle(%__MODULE__{icon: branch} = args)
    when branch in [:green_line_b, :green_line_c, :green_line_d, :green_line_e] do
  "green_line_" <> letter = Atom.to_string(branch)
  svg_icon_with_circle(%{args | icon: %Route{id: "Green-" <> String.upcase(letter), type: 0}})
end
def svg_icon_with_circle(%__MODULE__{icon: mode} = args)
    when mode in [:subway, :bus, :logan_express, :massport_shuttle, :commuter_rail, :ferry] do
  mode |> ViewHelpers.mode_icon(args.size) |> do_svg_icon_with_circle(args)
end
def svg_icon_with_circle(%__MODULE__{icon: :parking_lot} = args) do
  ViewHelpers.fa("square-parking") |> do_svg_icon_with_circle(args)
end
def svg_icon_with_circle(%__MODULE__{icon: icon, size: size} = args)
    when icon in [
           :calendar,
           :stop,
           :station,
           :access,
           :no_access,
           :silver_line,
           :the_ride,
           :reversal,
           :variant,
           :t_logo,
           :service_regular,
           :service_storm,
           :service_none
         ] do
  "icon-#{icon_name(icon)}-#{size}.svg" |> ViewHelpers.svg() |> do_svg_icon_with_circle(args)
end
elixir_type: (%{:icon => dynamic(), :size => :default or :small, :show_tooltip? => :false or :true, :aria_hidden? => :false or :true, :__struct__ => :"DotcomWeb.PartialView.SvgIconWithCircle"} -> dynamic())

def time_filter(filter, current_timeframe, path_opts) do
  path_method = Keyword.fetch!(path_opts, :method)
  item = Keyword.fetch!(path_opts, :item) |> get_item_value()
  path_params = path_opts |> Keyword.get(:params, []) |> time_filter_params(filter)

  path =
    apply(DotcomWeb.Router.Helpers, path_method, [DotcomWeb.Endpoint, :show, item, path_params])

  filter
  |> time_filter_text()
  |> link(class: time_filter_class(filter, current_timeframe), to: path)
end
elixir_type: (atom(), atom(), empty_list() or non_empty_list({atom(), term()}, empty_list()) -> dynamic())

      :div,
      gettext(
        "We can only provide trip data for the %{rating} schedule, valid until %{end_date}.",
        rating: rating_name(error),
        end_date: rating_end_date(error)
      )
    )
  ]
end
def no_trips_message(_, direction, date) when not is_nil(direction) and not is_nil(date) do
  gettext("There are no scheduled %{direction} trips on %{date}.",
    direction: downcase_direction(direction),
    date: format_full_date(date)
  )
end
def no_trips_message(_, direction, nil) when not is_nil(direction) do
  gettext("There are no scheduled %{direction} trips.", direction: downcase_direction(direction))
end
def no_trips_message(
      _,
      _,
      date
    )
    when not is_nil(date) do
  gettext("There are no scheduled trips on %{date}.", date: format_full_date(date))
end
def no_trips_message(_, _, _) do
  ~t"There are no scheduled trips."
end

definition:
def pretty_route_name(route) do
  route_prefix =
    if route.type == 3 do
      "Route "
    else
      ""
    end

  route_name =
    route.name
    |> String.replace_trailing(" Line", " line")
    |> String.replace_trailing(" Ferry", " ferry")
    |> String.replace_trailing(" Trolley", " trolley")
    |> break_text_at_slash

  route_prefix <> route_name
end

definition:
def single_trip_fares(%Route{fare_class: :free_fare} = route) do
  route
  |> to_fare_summary_atom()
  |> mode_summaries()
  |> Enum.find(fn summary -> summary.duration == :single_trip end)
  |> (&(&1.fares || [])).()
  |> Enum.map(fn fare -> {elem(fare, 0), ~t"Free"} end)
end
def single_trip_fares(route) do
  route
  |> to_fare_summary_atom()
  |> mode_summaries()
  |> Enum.find(fn summary -> summary.duration == :single_trip end)
  |> (&(&1.fares || [])).()
end

def render_filter_option(form, type, option) do
  id = "#{type}_#{option.value}"
  name = "search[#{type}][#{option.value}]"

  content_tag(:li) do
    label(form, type, for: id, class: "facet-label") do
      [
        content_tag(:input, "",
          type: "checkbox",
          id: id,
          name: name,
          value: "true",
          checked: option.active?
        ),
        content_tag(:span, "#{option.label} (#{option.count})")
      ]
    end
  end
end

def end_session_uri(config, params \\ %{}) do
  discovery_document_uri = config.discovery_document_uri

  with {:ok, document} <- Document.fetch_document(discovery_document_uri) do
    if end_session_endpoint = document.end_session_endpoint do
      params = Map.merge(%{client_id: config.client_id}, params)
      {:ok, build_uri(end_session_endpoint, params)}
    else
      {:error, :endpoint_not_set}
    end
  end
end

definition:
def verify(config, jwt) do
  discovery_document_uri = config.discovery_document_uri

  with {:ok, protected} <- peek_protected(jwt),
       {:ok, decoded_protected} <- Jason.decode(protected),
       {:ok, token_alg} <- Map.fetch(decoded_protected, "alg"),
       {:ok, document} <- Document.fetch_document(discovery_document_uri),
       {true, claims, _jwk} <- verify_signature(document.jwks, token_alg, jwt),
       {:ok, unverified_claims} <- Jason.decode(claims),
       {:ok, verified_claims} <- verify_claims(unverified_claims, config) do
    {:ok, verified_claims}
  else
    {:error, %Jason.DecodeError{}} ->
      {:error, {:invalid_jwt, "token claims did not contain a JSON payload"}}

    {:error, :peek_protected} ->
      {:error, {:invalid_jwt, "invalid token format"}}

    {:error, invalid_claim, message} ->
      {:error, {:invalid_jwt, "invalid #{invalid_claim} claim: #{message}"}}

    :error ->
      {:error, {:invalid_jwt, "no `alg` found in token"}}

    {false, _claims, _jwk} ->
      {:error, {:invalid_jwt, "verification failed"}}

    {:error, {:case_clause, _}} ->
      {:error, {:invalid_jwt, "verification failed"}}

    other ->
      other
  end
end
elixir_type: (%{:leeway => if_set(integer()), :scope => empty_list() or non_empty_list(binary(), empty_list()) or binary(), :response_type => empty_list() or non_empty_list(binary(), empty_list()) or binary(), :client_secret => binary(), :client_id => binary(), :discovery_document_uri => binary()}, binary() -> {:ok, open_map()} or {:error, term()})
spec: @spec verify(config(), jwt :: String.t()) :: {:ok, claims :: map()} | {:error, term()}

definition:
def get_route_accuracy(route_id) do
  today = Date.utc_today()
  yesterday = today |> Timex.shift(days: -1) |> Date.to_iso8601()
  previous_day = today |> Timex.shift(days: -2) |> Date.to_iso8601()

  {yesterday_query, _} =
    PredictionAccuracy.filter(%{
      "route_ids" => route_id,
      "chart_range" => "Hourly",
      "service_date" => yesterday
    })

  yesterday_accs =
    yesterday_query
    |> Filters.stats_by_chart_range(%{"chart_range" => "Hourly"})
    |> where(environment: "prod")
    |> PredictionAnalyzer.Repo.all()

  {previous_day_query, _} =
    PredictionAccuracy.filter(%{
      "route_ids" => route_id,
      "chart_range" => "Hourly",
      "service_date" => previous_day
    })

  previous_day_accs =
    previous_day_query
    |> Filters.stats_by_chart_range(%{"chart_range" => "Hourly"})
    |> where(environment: "prod")
    |> PredictionAnalyzer.Repo.all()

  yesterday_accuracy = get_accuracy(yesterday_accs)
  previous_day_accuracy = get_accuracy(previous_day_accs)

  if yesterday_accuracy < previous_day_accuracy - @drop_threshold do
    Logger.warning(
      "accuracy_drop on #{route_id} from #{previous_day_accuracy} to #{yesterday_accuracy} between #{previous_day} and #{yesterday}"
    )
  end

  [yesterday_accuracy: yesterday_accuracy, previous_day_accuracy: previous_day_accuracy]
end
elixir_type: (binary() -> empty_list() or non_empty_list({:yesterday_accuracy, float()} or {:previous_day_accuracy, float()}, empty_list()))

def do_aggregation(current_time, repo) do
  try do
    repo.transaction(
      fn ->
        for environment <- ~w(prod dev-green dev-blue),
            kind <- [nil | Map.values(Filters.kinds())],
            {bin_name, {bin_min, bin_max, bin_error_min, bin_error_max}} <- Filters.bins(),
            in_next_two? <- [true, false] do
          {:ok, r} =
            Query.calculate_aggregate_accuracy(
              repo,
              current_time,
              kind,
              in_next_two?,
              bin_name,
              bin_min,
              bin_max,
              bin_error_min,
              bin_error_max,
              environment
            )

          Logger.info(
            "prediction_accuracy_aggregator #{environment} #{kind} #{bin_name} result=#{inspect(r)}"
          )
        end
      end,
      timeout: 5 * 60 * 1000
    )

    :ok
  rescue
    e in DBConnection.ConnectionError ->
      Logger.warning("#{__MODULE__} do_aggregation #{inspect(e)}")
      :error
  end
end
elixir_type: (%{:year => integer(), :month => integer(), :day => integer(), :calendar => atom(), :hour => integer(), :minute => integer(), :second => integer(), :microsecond => {integer(), integer()}, :time_zone => dynamic(), :zone_abbr => dynamic(), :utc_offset => integer(), :std_offset => integer(), :__struct__ => :"DateTime"}, atom() -> :ok or :error)

def do_aggregation_with_retries(
      current_time,
      repo,
      retry_time_fetcher,
      retries \\ @max_retries
    ) do
  case do_aggregation(current_time, repo) do
    :ok ->
      :ok

    :error ->
      if retries > 0 do
        retry_ms = retry_time_fetcher.(retries)
        Logger.info("Prediction aggregation failed, retrying in #{retry_ms / 1000} seconds")
        Process.sleep(retry_ms)
        do_aggregation_with_retries(current_time, repo, retry_time_fetcher, retries - 1)
      else
        :error
      end
  end
end
elixir_type: (%{:year => integer(), :month => integer(), :day => integer(), :calendar => atom(), :hour => integer(), :minute => integer(), :second => integer(), :microsecond => {integer(), integer()}, :time_zone => dynamic(), :zone_abbr => dynamic(), :utc_offset => integer(), :std_offset => integer(), :__struct__ => :"DateTime"}, atom(), (integer() -> integer()), integer() -> :ok or :error)

def calculate_aggregate_accuracy(
      repo_module,
      current_time,
      kind,
      in_next_two?,
      bin_name,
      bin_min,
      bin_max,
      bin_error_min,
      bin_error_max,
      environment
    ) do
  {service_date, hour_of_day, minute_of_hour, min_unix, max_unix} =
    current_time
    |> Timex.shift(minutes: -Application.get_env(:prediction_analyzer, :analysis_lookback_min))
    |> PredictionAnalyzer.Utilities.service_date_info()

  Logger.info("insert_accuracy_query_start")

  repo_module.query(
    insert_accuracy_query(kind),
    [
      service_date,
      hour_of_day,
      bin_name,
      bin_min,
      bin_max,
      bin_error_min,
      bin_error_max,
      min_unix,
      max_unix,
      environment,
      kind,
      in_next_two?,
      minute_of_hour
    ],
    telemetry_event: repo_module.config()[:telemetry_prefix] ++ [:named_query],
    telemetry_options: [name: :insert_accuracy]
  )
end
elixir_type: (atom(), %{:year => integer(), :month => integer(), :day => integer(), :calendar => atom(), :hour => integer(), :minute => integer(), :second => integer(), :microsecond => {integer(), integer()}, :time_zone => dynamic(), :zone_abbr => dynamic(), :utc_offset => integer(), :std_offset => integer(), :__struct__ => :"DateTime"}, binary(), :true or :false, binary(), integer(), integer(), integer(), integer(), binary() -> {:ok, term()} or :error)

def calculate_nth_at_stop(predictions) do
  predictions
  |> Enum.group_by(& &1.stop_id)
  |> Enum.flat_map(fn {stop_id, predictions} ->
    if !is_nil(stop_id) do
      predictions
      |> Enum.sort_by(
        &{if &1.stops_away == 0 do
           0
         else
           1
         end, &1.arrival_time || &1.departure_time}
      )
      |> Enum.with_index(1)
      |> Enum.map(fn {prediction, index} -> %{prediction | nth_at_stop: index} end)
    else
      predictions
    end
  end)
end
elixir_type: (empty_list() or non_empty_list(open_map(), empty_list()) -> empty_list() or non_empty_list(open_map(), empty_list()))

def get_commuter_rail_predictions() do
  url_path = "predictions"

  params = %{
    "filter[route]" =>
      :commuter_rail |> PredictionAnalyzer.Utilities.routes_for_mode() |> Enum.join(","),
    "include" => "vehicle"
  }

  case PredictionAnalyzer.Utilities.APIv3.request(url_path, params: params) do
    {:ok, %{body: body, headers: headers}} ->
      last_modified = headers |> Enum.into(%{}) |> Map.get("last-modified")
      body |> Jason.decode!() |> store_commuter_rail_predictions(last_modified)

    {:error, e} ->
      Logger.warning("Could not download commuter rail predictions; received: #{inspect(e)}")
      %{}
  end
end

def ms_to_next_hour(local_now \\ Timex.local()) do
  local_now
  |> Timex.shift(hours: 1)
  |> Timex.set(minute: 0, second: 30)
  |> Timex.diff(local_now, :milliseconds)
end
elixir_type: (%{:year => integer(), :month => integer(), :day => integer(), :calendar => atom(), :hour => integer(), :minute => integer(), :second => integer(), :microsecond => {integer(), integer()}, :time_zone => dynamic(), :zone_abbr => dynamic(), :utc_offset => integer(), :std_offset => integer(), :__struct__ => :"DateTime"} -> integer())

definition:
def ms_to_next_week(local_now \\ Timex.local()) do
  days_to_end_of_week = local_now |> Timex.days_to_end_of_week(:sun)

  case days_to_end_of_week do
    0 ->
      local_now
      |> Timex.shift(days: 7)
      |> Timex.set(hour: 1, minute: 0, second: 0)
      |> Timex.diff(local_now, :milliseconds)

    n ->
      local_now
      |> Timex.shift(days: n)
      |> Timex.set(hour: 1, minute: 0, second: 0)
      |> Timex.diff(local_now, :milliseconds)
  end
end
elixir_type: (%{:year => integer(), :month => integer(), :day => integer(), :calendar => atom(), :hour => integer(), :minute => integer(), :second => integer(), :microsecond => {integer(), integer()}, :time_zone => dynamic(), :zone_abbr => dynamic(), :utc_offset => integer(), :std_offset => integer(), :__struct__ => :"DateTime"} -> integer())

def compare(new_vehicles, old_vehicles) do
  Enum.each(new_vehicles, fn {_id, new_vehicle} ->
    compare_vehicle(new_vehicle, old_vehicles[new_vehicle.id])
  end)

  new_vehicle_ids = new_vehicles |> Map.keys() |> MapSet.new()
  old_vehicle_ids = old_vehicles |> Map.keys() |> MapSet.new()
  lost_vehicle_ids = MapSet.difference(old_vehicle_ids, new_vehicle_ids)

  lost_vehicle_ids
  |> Enum.group_by(&old_vehicles[&1].environment)
  |> Enum.each(fn {environment, vehicle_ids} ->
    Logger.info(
      [
        "vehicles_dropped_from_feed",
        "environment=#{environment}",
        "count=#{length(vehicle_ids)}"
        | vehicle_ids |> Enum.map(&"vehicle=#{old_vehicles[&1].label}")
      ]
      |> Enum.intersperse(" ")
    )
  end)

  new_vehicles
end
elixir_type: (%{dynamic() => if_set(dynamic())}, %{dynamic() => if_set(dynamic())} -> %{dynamic() => if_set(dynamic())})

def parse_vehicles(%{"entity" => entities}, environment) do
  Enum.flat_map(entities, fn e ->
    case Vehicle.from_json(e, environment) do
      {:ok, vehicle} ->
        [vehicle]

      :ignore ->
        []

      _ ->
        Logger.warning("failed_to_parse_vehicle_entity #{inspect(e)}")
        []
    end
  end)
end
def parse_vehicles(_, _) do
  []
end

def request(path, extra_headers \\ [], opts) do
  base_url = Application.get_env(:prediction_analyzer, :api_base_url)

  headers =
    extra_headers ++ api_key_headers(Application.get_env(:prediction_analyzer, :api_v3_key))

  http_fetcher = Application.get_env(:prediction_analyzer, :http_fetcher)

  with {:ok, req} <-
         http_fetcher.get(
           base_url <> path,
           headers,
           Keyword.merge(@default_opts, opts)
         ),
       %{status_code: 200} <- req do
    {:ok, req}
  else
    e -> {:error, e}
  end
end
elixir_type: (binary(), empty_list() or non_empty_list({binary(), binary()}, empty_list()), empty_list() or non_empty_list({atom(), term()}, empty_list()) -> {:error, term()} or {:ok, open_map()})

def build_request({direction_ids, station_ids}) do
  id_filter = station_ids |> Enum.map(&URI.encode/1) |> Enum.join(",")
  direction_filter = direction_ids |> Enum.map(&URI.encode/1) |> Enum.join(",")
  schedule_api_url = Application.get_env(:realtime_signs, :api_v3_url) <> "/schedules"
  schedule_api_url <> "?filter[stop]=#{id_filter}&filter[direction_id]=#{direction_filter}"
end
elixir_type: ({empty_list() or non_empty_list(binary(), empty_list()), empty_list() or non_empty_list(binary(), empty_list())} -> binary())

def get_schedules(station_ids) do
  http_client = Application.get_env(:realtime_signs, :http_client)
  api_v3_key = Application.get_env(:realtime_signs, :api_v3_key)

  results =
    Enum.group_by(station_ids, &directions_for_station_id/1)
    |> Enum.map(&build_request/1)
    |> Enum.map(
      &http_client.get(&1, api_key_header(api_v3_key), timeout: 5000, recv_timeout: 5000)
    )
    |> Enum.map(&validate_and_parse_response/1)

  if Enum.any?(results, &(&1 == :error)) do
    :error
  else
    Enum.concat(results)
  end
end
elixir_type: (empty_list() or non_empty_list(binary(), empty_list()) -> empty_list() or non_empty_list(%{}, empty_list()) or :error)

def parse_body(body) do
  case Jason.decode(body) do
    {:ok, response} ->
      Map.get(response, "data")

    {:error, reason} ->
      Logger.warning("Could not decode response for scheduled headways: #{inspect(reason)}")
      []
  end
end

def parse(line) do
  with [metadata, message] <- String.split(line, ";"),
       [priority_str, sequence_str, timestamp_str, flag] <- String.split(metadata, ",", parts: 4),
       {priority_int, ""} <- Integer.parse(priority_str),
       {sequence, ""} <- Integer.parse(sequence_str),
       {timestamp, ""} <- Integer.parse(timestamp_str),
       {:ok, facility, severity} <- SyslogParser.decode_priority(priority_int) do
    {:ok,
     %{
       facility: facility,
       severity: severity,
       message: message,
       timestamp: timestamp,
       sequence: sequence,
       flags: parse_flags(flag)
     }}
  else
    _ -> {:error, :parse_error}
  end
end
elixir_type: (binary() -> {:ok, %{:flags => empty_list() or non_empty_list(atom(), empty_list()), :sequence => integer(), :timestamp => integer(), :message => binary(), :severity => :debug or :info or :notice or :warn or :warning or :error or :critical or :alert or :emergency, :facility => :local7 or :local6 or :local5 or :local4 or :local3 or :local2 or :local1 or :local0 or :log_alert or :log_audit or :NTP or :FTP or :clock or :UUCP or :network_news or :line_printer or :syslogd or :security_authorization or :system or :mail or :user_level or :kernel}} or {:error, :parse_error})

def decode_priority(priority) when priority >= 0 and priority <= 191 do
  facility = div(priority, 8)
  severity = Integer.mod(priority, 8)
  {:ok, facility_name(facility), severity_name(severity)}
end
def decode_priority(_priority) do
  {:error, :parse_error}
end
elixir_type: (integer() -> {:ok, :kernel or :user_level or :mail or :system or :security_authorization or :syslogd or :line_printer or :network_news or :UUCP or :clock or :security_authorization or :FTP or :NTP or :log_audit or :log_alert or :clock or :local0 or :local1 or :local2 or :local3 or :local4 or :local5 or :local6 or :local7, :emergency or :alert or :critical or :error or :warning or :warn or :notice or :info or :debug} or {:error, :parse_error})

def parse(<<"<", pri, ">", message::binary>>) when pri >= 48 and pri <= 57 do
  do_parse(pri - 48, message)
end
def parse(<<"<", pri0, pri1, ">", message::binary>>)
    when pri0 >= 49 and pri0 <= 57 and pri1 >= 48 and pri1 <= 57 do
  do_parse((pri0 - 48) * 10 + (pri1 - 48), message)
end
def parse(<<"<", 49, pri0, pri1, ">", message::binary>>)
    when pri0 >= 48 and pri0 <= 57 and pri1 >= 48 and pri1 <= 57 do
  do_parse(100 + (pri0 - 48) * 10 + (pri1 - 48), message)
end
def parse(_) do
  {:error, :parse_error}
end
elixir_type: (binary() -> {:ok, %{:message => binary(), :severity => :debug or :info or :notice or :warn or :warning or :error or :critical or :alert or :emergency, :facility => :local7 or :local6 or :local5 or :local4 or :local3 or :local2 or :local1 or :local0 or :log_alert or :log_audit or :NTP or :FTP or :clock or :UUCP or :network_news or :line_printer or :syslogd or :security_authorization or :system or :mail or :user_level or :kernel}} or {:error, :parse_error})

def active_application_partition_cell() do
  label = "Part usage"
  app_partition_path = KV.get_active("nerves_fw_application_part0_devpath")

  with true <- devpath_specified?(app_partition_path),
       {:ok, stats} <- runtime_mod().filesystem_stats(app_partition_path) do
    text = :io_lib.format("~p MB (~p%)", [stats.used_mb, stats.used_percent])

    if stats.used_percent < 85 do
      {label, text}
    else
      {label, [:red, text]}
    end
  else
    _ -> {label, [:red, "not available"]}
  end
end
elixir_type: ( -> {binary(), empty_list() or non_empty_list(integer() or atom() or binary() or dynamic(), binary() or atom() or empty_list()) or atom() or binary()})

def applications_cell(apps) do
  not_started = apps[:expected] -- apps[:started]
  started_count = length(apps[:started])

  if not_started == [] do
    {"Applications", "#{started_count} started"}
  else
    {"Applications",
     [:yellow, "#{started_count} started (#{Enum.join(not_started, ", ")} not started)"]}
  end
end
elixir_type: (%{:loaded => empty_list() or non_empty_list(term(), empty_list()), :started => empty_list() or non_empty_list(term(), empty_list()), :expected => empty_list() or non_empty_list(term(), empty_list())} -> {binary(), empty_list() or non_empty_list(integer() or atom() or binary() or dynamic(), binary() or atom() or empty_list()) or atom() or binary()})
spec: @spec applications_cell(%{expected: list(), started: list(), loaded: list()}) :: cell()

def extra_rows(opts) do
  case opts[:extra_rows] do
    fun when is_function(fun, 0) -> fun.()
    {m, f, args} -> apply(m, f, args)
    rows when is_list(rows) -> rows
    _ -> []
  end
catch
  err, msg -> [[{[:red, ":extra_rows failed"], ["failed (", inspect(err), ") - ", inspect(msg)]}]]
end

    [] ->
      []

    addresses ->
      formatted_list =

      [{name, formatted_list}]
  end
end

def print(opts \\ []) do
  combined_opts = Application.get_all_env(:nerves_motd) |> Keyword.merge(opts)
  apps = runtime_mod().applications()

  if ready?(apps) do
    [
      logo(combined_opts),
      IO.ANSI.reset(),
      firmware_designation(),
      "\n",
      Enum.map(rows(apps, combined_opts), &format_row/1),
      "\n",
      "Nerves CLI help: https://hexdocs.pm/nerves/iex-with-nerves.html\n"
    ]
    |> IO.ANSI.format()
    |> IO.puts()
  end

  :ok
rescue
  error -> IO.puts("Could not print MOTD: #{inspect(error)}")
end

definition:
def uptime() do
  {uptime, _} = :erlang.statistics(:wall_clock)
  {d, {h, m, s}} = :calendar.seconds_to_daystime(div(uptime, 1000))

  days =
    if d > 0 do
      :io_lib.format("~b days, ", [d])
    else
      []
    end

  hours =
    if d + h > 0 do
      :io_lib.format("~b hours, ", [h])
    else
      []
    end

  minutes =
    if d + h + m > 0 do
      :io_lib.format("~b minutes and ", [m])
    else
      []
    end

  seconds = :io_lib.format("~b", [s])

  millis =
    if d + h + m == 0 do
      :io_lib.format(".~3..0b", [rem(uptime, 1000)])
    else
      []
    end

  [days, hours, minutes, seconds, millis, " seconds"]
end
elixir_type: ( -> binary() or empty_list() or non_empty_list(integer() or dynamic(), binary() or empty_list()))

def extract_ifaddr_addresses(kv_pairs, acc \\ [])
def extract_ifaddr_addresses([], acc) do

def extract_ifaddr_addresses([{:addr, addr}, {:netmask, netmask} | rest], acc) do
  extract_ifaddr_addresses(rest, [{addr, netmask} | acc])
end
def extract_ifaddr_addresses([_other | rest], acc) do
  extract_ifaddr_addresses(rest, acc)
end

definition:
def word(index) when index in 0..255 do
  m = rem(index, 5)
  len = m + 3
  offset = div(index, 5) * 25 + div(m * (m + 5), 2)
  binary_part(@word34567_words, offset, len)
end
elixir_type: (integer() -> binary())

definition:
def cmd(cmd, params, log_level_or_return) do
  case System.find_executable(cmd) do
    nil ->
      Logger.error(
        "Executable #{cmd} was not found. The Nerves System must be fixed to include it!"
      )

      {"", 255}

    cmd_path ->
      run_cmd(cmd_path, params, log_level_or_return)
  end
end
elixir_type: (binary(), empty_list() or non_empty_list(binary(), empty_list()), :debug or :info or :warn or :error or :return -> {dynamic(), integer()})

definition:
def firmware_slots(opts \\ []) do
  case FwupOps.status(opts) do
    {:ok, status} ->
      status

    {:error, reason} ->
      Logger.info("Using slot detection heuristic due to ops.fw error: #{inspect(reason)}")
      active = KV.get("nerves_fw_active") || "a"

      next =
        if firmware_valid?() do
          active
        else
          opposite_slot(active)
        end

      %{active: active, next: next}
  end
end
elixir_type: (empty_list() or non_empty_list({:devpath, binary()} or {:fwup_env, %{binary() => if_set(binary())}} or {:fwup_extra_options, empty_list() or non_empty_list(binary(), empty_list())} or {:fwup_path, binary()} or {:ops_fw_path, binary()} or {:reboot, :true or :false}, empty_list()) -> %{:next => binary(), :active => binary()})

definition:
def get_expected_started_apps() do
  {:ok, [[boot]]} = :init.get_argument(:boot)
  contents = File.read!("#{boot}.boot")
  {:script, _name, instructions} = :erlang.binary_to_term(contents)

  apps =
    for {:apply, {:application, :start_boot, [app | _]}} <- instructions do
      app
    end

  {:ok, apps}
rescue
  e ->
    Logger.error("get_expected_started_apps encountered unexpected error: #{inspect(e)}")
    :error
end
elixir_type: ( -> {:ok, empty_list() or non_empty_list(atom(), empty_list())} or :error)

def validate_firmware(opts \\ []) do
  with {:error, reason} <- FwupOps.validate(opts) do
    Logger.error("Using old validation logic due to ops.fw error: #{inspect(reason)}")

    if KV.get("upgrade_available") do
      KV.put(%{"upgrade_available" => "0", "bootcount" => "0", "nerves_fw_validated" => "1"})
    else
      KV.put("nerves_fw_validated", "1")
    end
  end
end

def factory_reset(opts \\ []) do
  reboot? = Keyword.get(opts, :reboot, true)

  with {:ok, _} <- call_run_fwup("factory-reset", opts) do
    if reboot? do
      Heart.guarded_immediate_reboot()
    else
      :ok
    end
  end
end

definition:
def do_heart_check(uptime_minutes) do
  cond do
    uptime_minutes >= @give_up_minutes ->
      Logger.error("Took too long to validate firmware. Rebooting.")
      :error

    uptime_minutes < @start_warning_minutes ->
      :ok

    uptime_minutes != Process.get(:last_warning_minutes) ->
      Logger.warning(
        "Firmware not validated. Check logs. Rebooting in #{@give_up_minutes - uptime_minutes} minutes if unfixed."
      )

      Process.put(:last_warning_minutes, uptime_minutes)
      :ok

    true ->
      :ok
  end
end
elixir_type: (integer() -> :ok or :error)

definition:
def heart_check() do
  uptime_minutes = get_uptime_minutes()
  do_heart_check(uptime_minutes)
end
elixir_type: ( -> :ok or :error)

definition:
def run(opts) do
  retry_delay = Keyword.get(opts, :retry_delay, @retry_delay)
  :ok = :heart.set_callback(__MODULE__, :heart_check)
  Heart.init_complete()

  {:ok, expected_apps} =
    repeat_while(
      &Nerves.Runtime.get_expected_started_apps/0,
      :error,
      10,
      retry_delay,
      "getting expected apps"
    )

  repeat_until(
    fn -> all_applications_started?(expected_apps) end,
    10,
    retry_delay,
    "waiting for apps to start"
  )

  status =
    repeat_while(
      &Nerves.Runtime.firmware_validation_status/0,
      :unknown,
      10,
      retry_delay,
      "getting firmware validation status"
    )

  if status == :unvalidated do
    Logger.info("Firmware not validated. Validating now...")
    :ok = Nerves.Runtime.validate_firmware()
    Logger.info("Firmware validated successfully")
  else
    Logger.info("Firmware valid and all applications started successfully")
  end

  :heart.clear_callback()
end
elixir_type: (empty_list() or non_empty_list({atom(), term()}, empty_list()) -> :ok)

def file_to_map(file) do
  Defconfig
  |> struct()
  |> Map.put(:path, file)
  |> Map.put(
    :config,
    File.read!(file)
    |> String.trim()
    |> String.split("\n")
    |> strip_comments()
    |> Map.new(fn line ->
      [key, ugly_val] = String.split(line, "=", parts: 2)
      {String.trim(key), parse_val(ugly_val)}
    end)
  )
end
elixir_type: (dynamic() or empty_list() or non_empty_list(integer() or dynamic(), dynamic() or empty_list()) -> %{:path => dynamic(), :config => %{binary() => if_set(:true or :false or binary() or empty_list() or non_empty_list(binary(), empty_list()))}, :rules => non_empty_list(%{:check => dynamic(), :args => dynamic(), :__struct__ => :"Nerves.System.Linter.Rule"}, empty_list()) or empty_list(), :errors => non_empty_list(dynamic(), empty_list()) or empty_list(), :success => non_empty_list(dynamic(), empty_list()) or empty_list(), :warnings => non_empty_list(dynamic(), empty_list()) or empty_list(), :__struct__ => :"Nerves.System.Defconfig"})

  |> String.trim()
  |> String.split("\"")

  |> maybe_parse_bool()
  |> maybe_parse_val_as_list()

def strip_comment_from_line(line, acc \\ <<>>)
def strip_comment_from_line(<<>>, acc) do
  acc
end
def strip_comment_from_line(<<"#", _rest::binary>>, _acc) do
  nil
end
def strip_comment_from_line(<<char, rest::binary>>, acc) do

end

definition:
def strip_comments(data, acc \\ [])
def strip_comments([], acc) do
  Enum.reverse(acc)
end
def strip_comments([line | rest], acc) do
  case strip_comment_from_line(line) do
    nil -> strip_comments(rest, acc)
    "" -> strip_comments(rest, acc)
    stripped -> strip_comments(rest, [stripped | acc])
  end
end
elixir_type: (empty_list() or non_empty_list(binary(), empty_list()), empty_list() or non_empty_list(binary(), empty_list()) -> empty_list() or non_empty_list(binary(), empty_list()))

def match(table, pattern) do
  registry = PropertyTable.Supervisor.registry_name(table)
  {:ok, matcher} = Registry.meta(registry, :matcher)

  :ets.foldl(
    fn {property, value, _timestamp}, acc ->
      if matcher.matches?(pattern, property) do
        [{property, value} | acc]
      else
        acc
      end
    end,
    [],
    table
  )
end
elixir_type: (atom(), term() -> empty_list() or non_empty_list({term(), term()}, empty_list()))

definition:
def start_link(options) do
  name =
    case Keyword.fetch(options, :name) do
      {:ok, name} when is_atom(name) -> name
      {:ok, other} -> raise ArgumentError, "expected :name to be an atom, got: #{inspect(other)}"
      :error -> raise ArgumentError, "expected :name option to be present"
    end

  tuple_events = Keyword.get(options, :tuple_events, false)

  if not is_boolean(tuple_events) do
    raise ArgumentError, "expected :tuple_events to be boolean, got: #{inspect(tuple_events)}"
  end

  default_transformer =
    if tuple_events do
      &PropertyTable.Event.to_tuple/1
    else
      &Function.identity/1
    end

  event_transformer = Keyword.get(options, :event_transformer, default_transformer)

  if not is_function(event_transformer, 1) do
    raise ArgumentError,
          "expected :event_transformer to a function, got: #{inspect(event_transformer)}"
  end

  matcher = Keyword.get(options, :matcher, PropertyTable.Matcher.StringPath)

  if not is_atom(matcher) do
    raise ArgumentError, "expected :matcher to be module, got: #{inspect(matcher)}"
  end

  properties = Keyword.get(options, :properties, [])

  if !Enum.all?(properties, fn {k, _} -> matcher.check_property(k) == :ok end) do
    raise ArgumentError,
          "expected :properties to contain valid properties, got: #{inspect(properties)}"
  end

  persistence_options = maybe_get_persistence_options(options)

  Supervisor.start_link(
    __MODULE__.Supervisor,
    %{
      table: name,
      properties: properties,
      event_transformer: event_transformer,
      matcher: matcher,
      persistence_options: persistence_options
    },
    name: name
  )
end
elixir_type: (empty_list() or non_empty_list({:name, atom()} or {:properties, empty_list() or non_empty_list({term(), term()}, empty_list())} or {:tuple_events, :true or :false} or {:matcher, atom()} or {:persist_data_path, binary()} or {:persist_interval, integer()} or {:persist_max_snapshots, integer()} or {:persist_compression, integer()}, empty_list()) -> {:ok, pid()} or :ignore or {:error, {:already_started, pid()} or {:shutdown, term()} or term()})

def subscribe(table, pattern) do
  registry = PropertyTable.Supervisor.registry_name(table)
  {:ok, matcher} = Registry.meta(registry, :matcher)

  case matcher.check_pattern(pattern) do
    :ok ->
      {:ok, _} = Registry.register(registry, :subscriptions, pattern)
      :ok

    {:error, error} ->
      raise error
  end
end
elixir_type: (atom(), term() -> :ok)

def get_snapshot_list(options) do
  options = take_options(options)
  snapshot_path = snapshot_path(options)

  snapshots =
    File.ls!(snapshot_path)
    |> Enum.map(fn id ->
      stat = Path.join(snapshot_path, [id]) |> File.stat!()
      {id, stat.ctime}
    end)
    |> Enum.sort_by(fn {_id, ctime} -> ctime end)

  snapshots
end

def restore_from_disk(table, options, timestamp) do
  options = take_options(options)
  stable_path = data_file_path(options)
  backup_path = stable_path <> @data_backup_extension
  attempts = [stable_path, backup_path]

  result =
    Enum.reduce_while(attempts, {:error, :failed_to_load}, fn path, _ ->
      if File.exists?(path) do
        try_restore_tabfile(path)
      else
        Logger.warning("File #{path} does not exist! Trying a backup file...")
        {:cont, {:error, :enoent}}
      end
    end)

  case result do
    {:ok, data} ->
      ^table = :ets.new(table, [:named_table, :public])

      Enum.each(data, fn {property, value} -> :ets.insert(table, {property, value, timestamp}) end)

      :ok

    error ->
      error
  end
rescue
  e ->
    Logger.error("Failed to restore table from disk: #{inspect(e)}")
    {:error, e}
end
elixir_type: (atom(), empty_list() or non_empty_list({atom(), term()}, empty_list()), integer() -> :ok or {:error, atom()})

def restore_snapshot(options, snapshot_id) do
  options = take_options(options)
  stable_path = data_file_path(options)
  snapshot_path = snapshot_path(options, snapshot_id)
  _content = PersistFile.decode_file!(snapshot_path)
  Logger.debug("Restoring table file snapshot: #{snapshot_path} => #{stable_path}")
  _ = File.copy!(snapshot_path, stable_path)
  :ok
rescue
  e ->
    Logger.error("Failed to validate snapshot file! - #{inspect(e)}")
    {:error, e}
end
elixir_type: (empty_list() or non_empty_list({atom(), term()}, empty_list()), binary() -> :ok or {:error, %{atom() => if_set(term()), :__exception__ => :true, :__struct__ => atom()}})

definition:
def save_snapshot(table, options) do
  options = take_options(options)
  persist_to_disk!(table, options)
  snapshot_id = :crypto.strong_rand_bytes(8) |> Base.encode16()
  full_snapshot_name = "#{snapshot_id}"
  stable_path = data_file_path(options)
  snapshot_path = snapshot_path(options, full_snapshot_name)
  Logger.debug("Creating table file snapshot: #{snapshot_path}")
  File.copy!(stable_path, snapshot_path)
  maybe_clean_old_snapshots(options)
  {:ok, snapshot_id}
rescue
  e ->
    Logger.error("Failed to save snapshot to disk: #{inspect(e)}")
    {:error, e}
end
elixir_type: (atom(), empty_list() or non_empty_list({atom(), term()}, empty_list()) -> {:ok, binary()} or {:error, %{atom() => if_set(term()), :__exception__ => :true, :__struct__ => atom()}})

def encode_binary(table_content_binary) when is_binary(table_content_binary) do
  payload_length = byte_size(table_content_binary)
  payload_hash = :crypto.hash(:md5, table_content_binary)
  header = <<@magic_file_header::binary, @file_version::8, 0::8, payload_length::64>>
  [header, table_content_binary, payload_hash]
end
elixir_type: (binary() -> non_empty_list(binary(), empty_list()))

def create_ets_table(table, initial_properties, timestamp) do
  ^table = :ets.new(table, [:named_table, :public])

  Enum.each(initial_properties, fn {property, value} ->
    :ets.insert(table, {property, value, timestamp})
  end)
end
elixir_type: (atom(), empty_list() or non_empty_list({term(), term()}, empty_list()), integer() -> :ok)

def maybe_restore_ets_table(table_name, initial_properties, persistence_options, timestamp) do
  if :ets.info(table_name) != :undefined do
    :ets.delete(table_name)
  end

  case Persist.restore_from_disk(table_name, persistence_options, timestamp) do
    :ok ->
      Enum.each(initial_properties, fn {property, value} ->
        :ets.insert_new(table_name, {property, value, timestamp})
      end)

    {:error, _error_reason} ->
      create_ets_table(table_name, initial_properties, timestamp)
  end
end
elixir_type: (atom(), empty_list() or non_empty_list({term(), term()}, empty_list()), empty_list() or non_empty_list({atom(), term()}, empty_list()), integer() -> :ok)

    {:error, exception} -> raise exception
  end
end
elixir_type: (atom(), term(), term() -> :ok)

definition:
def put_many(table, properties) when is_list(properties) do
  timestamp = System.monotonic_time()

  timestamped_properties =
    for {property, value} <- properties do
      {property, value, timestamp}
    end

  case GenServer.call(server_name(table), {:put_many, timestamped_properties}) do
    :ok -> :ok
    {:error, exception} -> raise exception
  end
end
elixir_type: (atom(), empty_list() or non_empty_list({term(), term()}, empty_list()) -> :ok)

def broadcast(pubsub, topic, message, dispatcher \\ __MODULE__)
    when is_atom(pubsub) and is_atom(dispatcher) do
  topics = List.wrap(topic)
  {:ok, {topology, remote}} = Registry.meta(pubsub, :pubsub)
  broadcast = {:broadcast, topics, message, dispatcher}

  with :ok <- Firenest.Topology.broadcast(topology, remote, broadcast) do
    dispatch(pubsub, :none, topics, message, dispatcher)
  end
end
elixir_type: (atom(), term() or empty_list() or non_empty_list(term(), empty_list()), term(), atom() -> :ok or {:error, term()})

definition:
def broadcast!(pubsub, topic, message, dispatcher \\ __MODULE__) do
  case broadcast(pubsub, topic, message, dispatcher) do
    :ok -> :ok
    {:error, error} -> raise BroadcastError, "broadcast!/3 failed with #{inspect(error)}"
  end
end
elixir_type: (atom(), term() or empty_list() or non_empty_list(term(), empty_list()), term(), atom() -> :ok or none())

    when is_atom(pubsub) and is_pid(pid) and is_atom(dispatcher) do
  topics = List.wrap(topic)
  {:ok, {topology, remote}} = Registry.meta(pubsub, :pubsub)
  broadcast = {:broadcast, topics, message, dispatcher}

  with :ok <- Firenest.Topology.broadcast(topology, remote, broadcast) do
    dispatch(pubsub, pid, topics, message, dispatcher)
  end
end

def child_spec(options) do
  name = options[:name]
  {adapter, options} = Keyword.pop(options, :adapter)

  unless adapter && name do
    raise ArgumentError, "Firenest.Topology.child_spec/1 expects :adapter and :name as options"
  end

  adapter.child_spec(options)
end
elixir_type: (empty_list() or non_empty_list({atom(), term()}, empty_list()) -> %{:significant => if_set(:false or :true), :modules => if_set(:dynamic or non_empty_list(atom(), empty_list()) or empty_list()), :type => if_set(:worker or :supervisor), :shutdown => if_set(:infinity or integer() or :brutal_kill), :restart => if_set(:permanent or :transient or :temporary), :start => {atom(), atom(), empty_list() or non_empty_list(term(), empty_list())}, :id => term()})
